title: T-074 — shared rbac pool lifecycle (closeRbacPool) replacing per-call pool.end() (FT-014)
prompt:
# T-074 — shared rbac pool lifecycle (closeRbacPool) replacing per-call pool.end() (FT-014)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5.x
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-014** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P2** (S effort)

## Goal
Refactor packages/rbac/src/grant.ts to a shared pool + closeRbacPool exit hook.

## Implementation contract
1. Module-level `let pool: Pool | null = null` + `getRbacPool()` initialiser.
2. Export `closeRbacPool(): Promise<void>`.
3. Remove all internal pool.end() calls.
4. Test asserts pool reuse across consecutive grants + closeRbacPool re-init.

## Files (scope)
- packages/rbac/src/grant.ts [modify]
- packages/rbac/src/__tests__/grant-pool-lifecycle.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given two consecutive grantRole calls, when both run, then the same Pool reference is used.
2. Given closeRbacPool() is awaited, when the next grantRole runs, then a fresh Pool is created.
3. Given vitest afterAll, when closeRbacPool runs, then no `unhandled rejection` is logged from pg-pool.

## Test strategy
- RED: write grant-pool-lifecycle.test.ts asserting pool reuse + closeRbacPool re-init before refactor.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac test grant-pool-lifecycle`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not introduce a process.exit listener.
- Do not break grant-role correctness — only lifecycle changes.
- Do not use raw new pg.Pool.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Replace per-call pool.end() in packages/rbac/src/grant.ts with a shared pool lifecycle exposing closeRbacPool().
